definition.name: Zhentarim Mercenary
definition.description:
Ability Scores: Strength, Dexterity, Charisma
Feat: Zhentarim Ruffian
Skill Proficiencies: Intimidation and Perception
Tool Proficiency: Forgery Kit
Equipment: Choose A or B: (A) Club, Dagger, Forgery Kit, Fine Clothes, Hooded Lantern, Oil (3 flasks), 2 Pouches, String, Tinderbox, 11 GP; or (B) 50 GP

Maybe you needed the money. Maybe you longed for a family, no matter how dubious. Or maybe you’re just good at getting the job done by any means necessary. Whatever your reason, you enlisted with the Zhentarim, the most notorious mercenary guild in the Realms. Though the Zhentarim’s leaders insist the organization is more like a family than a shadowy syndicate, few families exhibit as much dishonesty, nepotism, and corruption as this one. You’ve honed your cunning, reflexes, and blade to climb the guild’s ranks.

race.description:
With ever-changing appearances, changelings reside in many societies undetected.

Changeling Traits
Fey, Changeling Instincts, Shape-Shifter
race.longDescription:
With ever-changing appearances, changelings reside in many societies undetected. Each changeling can supernaturally adopt any face they like. For some changelings, a new face may reveal an aspect of their soul.

The first changelings in the multiverse appeared in the Feywild, and the wondrous, mutable essence of that plane lingers in changelings today—even in those who have never set foot in the fey realm.

In their true form, changelings appear faded, their features almost devoid of detail. It is rare to see one in that form, for a typical changeling alters their shape the way others might change clothes. However, many changelings develop identities with more depth, crafting a persona complete with history and beliefs to go with each shape. A changeling adventurer might have personas for many situations, including negotiation, investigation, and combat.

Multiple changelings can share a persona. Personas can even pass down through a family, allowing a younger changeling to take advantage of contacts established by the persona’s previous users.

Changeling Traits

Creature Type: Fey
Size: Medium (about 4–7 feet tall) or Small (about 2–4 feet tall), chosen when you select this species
Speed: 30 feet

definition.name: Languages
definition.description:
Your character knows at least three languages: Common plus two languages you roll or choose from the Standard Languages table. Knowledge of a language means your character can communicate in it, read it, and write it. Your class and other features might also give you languages.

The Standard Languages table lists languages that are widespread on D&D worlds. Every player character knows Common, which originated in the planar metropolis of Sigil, the hub of the multiverse. The other standard languages originated with the first members of the most prominent species in the worlds of D&D and have since spread widely.

Standard Languages
1d12 Language Origin

3-4 Dwarvish Dwarves
5-6 Elvish Elves
7 Giant Giants

10-11 Halfling Halflings
12 Orc Orcs

definition.name: Changeling Instincts
definition.description: Thanks to your connection to the fey realm, you gain proficiency in two of the following skills of your choice: Deception, Insight, Intimidation, Performance, or Persuasion.
definition.snippet: Thanks to your connection to the fey realm, you gain proficiency in two of the following skills of your choice: Deception, Insight, Intimidation, Performance, or Persuasion.

definition.name: Shape-Shifter
definition.description:
As an action, you can shape-shift to change your appearance and your voice. You determine the specifics of the changes, including your coloration, hair length, and sex. You can also adjust your height and weight and can change your size between Medium and Small. You can make yourself appear as a member of another playable species, though none of your game statistics change. You can’t duplicate the appearance of an individual you’ve never seen, and you must adopt a form that has the same basic arrangement of limbs that you have. This trait doesn’t change your clothing and equipment.

While shape-shifted with this trait, you have Advantage on Charisma checks.

You stay in the new form until you take an action to revert to your true form.
definition.snippet: As an action, you can shape-shift to change your appearance and your voice and stay in the new form until you take an action to revert to your true form. Activate Shape-Shifter by clicking on this trait and selecting Shape-Shift. Deselect it when you take an action to stop its effects.

definition.name: Ability Score Increase
definition.description: When determining your character’s ability scores, increase one score by 2 and a different one by 1, or increase three scores by 1. None of these increases can raise a score above 20.

race.cardDescription: With ever-changing appearances, Changelings reside in many societies undetected.
race.highlights:
1. Change your appearance and voice at will, adopting any face you like.
  2. Read people closely and adapt your personas to gain trust—or slip past unnoticed.
  3. Reinvent yourself again and again, becoming whoever the moment calls for.

definition.name: Dagger
definition.description:
Proficiency with a Dagger allows you to add your proficiency bonus to the attack roll for any attack you make with it.

This weapon has the following mastery property. To use this property, you must have a feature that lets you use it.

Nick. When you make the extra attack of the Light property, you can make it as part of the Attack action instead of as a Bonus Action. You can make this extra attack only once per turn.

properties.name: Finesse
properties.description: When making an attack with a Finesse weapon, use your choice of your Strength or Dexterity modifier for the attack and damage rolls. You must use the same modifier for both rolls.

properties.name: Light
properties.description: When you take the [action]Attack[/action] action on your turn and attack with a Light weapon, you can make one extra attack as a Bonus Action later on the same turn. That extra attack must be made with a different Light weapon, and you don’t add your ability modifier to the extra attack’s damage unless that modifier is negative. For example, you can attack with a [items]Shortsword[/items] in one hand and a [items]Dagger[/items] in the other using the [action]Attack[/action] action and a Bonus Action, but you don’t add your Strength or Dexterity modifier to the damage roll of the Bonus Action unless that modifier is negative.

properties.name: Thrown
properties.description: If a weapon has the Thrown property, you can throw the weapon to make a ranged attack, and you can draw that weapon as part of the attack. If the weapon is a Melee weapon, use the same ability modifier for the attack and damage rolls that you use for a melee attack with that weapon.

properties.name: Versatile
properties.description: A Versatile weapon can be used with one or two hands. A damage value in parentheses appears with the property. The weapon deals that damage when used with two hands to make a melee attack.

properties.name: Sap
properties.description: If you hit a creature with this weapon, that creature has Disadvantage on its next attack roll before the start of your next turn.

definition.name: Lantern, Hooded
definition.description: A hooded lantern casts bright light in a 30-foot radius and dim light for an additional 30 feet. Once lit, it burns for 6 hours on a flask (1 pint) of oil. As an action, you can lower the hood, reducing the light to dim light in a 5-foot radius.

definition.name: Oil
definition.description:
You can douse a creature, object, or space with Oil or use it as fuel, as detailed below.

Dousing a Creature or an Object. When you take the Attack action, you can replace one of your attacks with throwing an Oil flask. Target one creature or object within 20 feet of yourself. The target must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw (DC 8 plus your Dexterity modifier and Proficiency Bonus) or be covered in oil. If the target takes Fire damage before the oil dries (after 1 minute), the target takes an extra 5 Fire damage from burning oil.

Dousing a Space. You can take the Utilize action to pour an Oil flask on level ground to cover a 5-foot-square area within 5 feet of yourself. If lit, the oil burns until the end of the turn 2 rounds from when the oil was lit (or 12 seconds) and deals 5 Fire damage to any creature that enters the area or ends its turn there. A creature can take this damage only once per turn.

Fuel. Oil serves as fuel for Lamps and Lanterns. Once lit, a flask of Oil burns for 6 hours in a Lamp or Lantern. That duration doesn’t need to be consecutive; you can extinguish the burning Oil (as a Utilize action) and rekindle it again until it has burned for a total of 6 hours.

definition.name: Alchemist's Supplies
definition.description:
Ability: Intelligence
Utilize: Identify a substance (DC 15), or start a fire (DC 15)
Craft: Acid, Alchemist’s Fire, Component Pouch, Oil, Paper, Perfume

If you have proficiency with a tool, add your Proficiency Bonus to any ability check you make that uses the tool. If you have proficiency in a skill that’s used with that check, you have Advantage on the check too.

definition.name: Backpack
definition.description: A Backpack holds up to 30 pounds within 1 cubic foot. It can also serve as a saddlebag.

definition.name: Pouch
definition.description: A Pouch holds up to 6 pounds within one-fifth of a cubic foot.

definition.name: Pouch
definition.description: A Pouch holds up to 6 pounds within one-fifth of a cubic foot.

definition.name: String
definition.description: String is 10 feet long. You can tie a knot in it as a Utilize action.

definition.name: Tinderbox
definition.description: A Tinderbox is a small container holding flint, fire steel, and tinder (usually dry cloth soaked in light oil) used to kindle a fire. Using it to light a Candle, Lamp, Lantern, or Torch—or anything else with exposed fuel—takes a Bonus Action. Lighting any other fire takes 1 minute.

definition.name: Bagpipes
definition.description:
Ability: Charisma
Utilize: Play a known tune (DC 10), or improvise a song (DC 15)

definition.name: Fine Clothes
definition.description: Fine Clothes are made of expensive fabrics and adorned with expertly crafted details. Some events and locations admit only people wearing these clothes.

definition.name: Forgery Kit
definition.description:
Ability: Dexterity
Utilize: Mimic 10 or fewer words of someone else’s handwriting (DC 15), or duplicate a wax seal (DC 20)

If you have proficiency with a tool, add your Proficiency Bonus to any ability check you make that uses the tool. If you have proficiency in a skill that’s used with that check, you have Advantage on the check too.

definition.name: Rations
definition.description: Rations consist of travel-ready food, including jerky, dried fruit, hardtack, and nuts. See “Malnutrition” for the risks of not eating.

definition.name: Rope
definition.description:
As a Utilize action, you can tie a knot with Rope if you succeed on a DC 10 Dexterity (Sleight of Hand) check. The Rope can be burst with a successful DC 20 Strength (Athletics) check.

You can bind an unwilling creature with the Rope only if the creature has the Grappled, Incapacitated, or Restrained condition. If the creature’s legs are bound, the creature has the Restrained condition until it escapes. Escaping the Rope requires the creature to make a successful DC 15 Dexterity (Acrobatics) check as an action.

definition.name: Bedroll
definition.description: A Bedroll sleeps one Small or Medium creature. While in a Bedroll, you automatically succeed on saving throws against extreme cold (see the Dungeon Master’s Guide).

definition.name: Tinderbox
definition.description: A Tinderbox is a small container holding flint, fire steel, and tinder (usually dry cloth soaked in light oil) used to kindle a fire. Using it to light a Candle, Lamp, Lantern, or Torch—or anything else with exposed fuel—takes a Bonus Action. Lighting any other fire takes 1 minute.

definition.name: Torch
definition.description: A Torch burns for 1 hour, casting Bright Light in a 20-foot radius and Dim Light for an additional 20 feet. When you take the Attack action, you can attack with the Torch, using it as a Simple Melee weapon. On a hit, the target takes 1 Fire damage.

definition.name: Waterskin
definition.description: A Waterskin holds up to 4 pints. If you don’t drink sufficient water, you risk dehydration.

definition.name: Monk
definition.description:
A Martial Artist of Supernatural Focus

Monks focus their internal reservoirs of power to create extraordinary, even supernatural, effects.

Primary Abilities: Dexterity & Wisdom
Hit Point Die: D8
Saves: Strength & Dexterity
definition.equipmentDescription:
Choose A or B:
(A) Spear, 5 Daggers, Artisan’s Tools or Musical Instrument chosen for the tool proficiency above, Explorer’s Pack, and 11 GP; or
(B) 50 GP

classFeatures.name: Core Monk Traits
classFeatures.description:
As a Level 1 Character:

  • Gain all the traits in the Core Monk Traits table.
  • Gain the Monk’s level 1 features.
Core Monk Traits
Primary Ability Dexterity and Wisdom
Hit Point Die D8 per Monk level
Saving Throw Proficiencies Strength and Dexterity
Skill Proficiencies Choose 2: Acrobatics, Athletics, History, Insight, Religion, or Stealth
Weapon Proficiencies Simple weapons and Martial weapons that have the Light property
Tool Proficiencies Choose one type of Artisan’s Tools or Musical Instrument
Armor Training None
Starting Equipment Choose A or B: (A) Spear, 5 Daggers, Artisan’s Tools or Musical Instrument chosen for the tool proficiency above, Explorer’s Pack, and 11 GP; or (B) 50 GP

classFeatures.name: Martial Arts
classFeatures.description:
Your practice of martial arts gives you mastery of combat styles that use your Unarmed Strike and Monk weapons, which are the following:

  • Simple Melee weapons
  • Martial Melee weapons that have the Light property

You gain the following benefits while you are unarmed or wielding only Monk weapons and you aren’t wearing armor or wielding a Shield.

Bonus Unarmed Strike. You can make an Unarmed Strike as a Bonus Action.

Martial Arts Die. You can roll 1d6 in place of the normal damage of your Unarmed Strike or Monk weapons. This die changes as you gain Monk levels, as shown in the Martial Arts column of the Monk Features table.

Dexterous Attacks. You can use your Dexterity modifier instead of your Strength modifier for the attack and damage rolls of your Unarmed Strikes and Monk weapons. In addition, when you use the Grapple or Shove option of your Unarmed Strike, you can use your Dexterity modifier instead of your Strength modifier to determine the save DC.
classFeatures.summary: Apply combat styles that use your Unarmed Strike and certain weapons.

classFeatures.name: Unarmored Defense
classFeatures.description: While you aren’t wearing armor or wielding a Shield, your base Armor Class equals 10 plus your Dexterity and Wisdom modifiers.
classFeatures.summary: Improve your base Armor Class when you aren't wearing any armor or wielding a Shield.

classFeatures.name: Monk’s Focus
classFeatures.description:
Your focus and martial training allow you to harness a well of extraordinary energy within yourself. This energy is represented by Focus Points. Your Monk level determines the number of points you have, as shown in the Focus Points column of the Monk Features table.

You can expend these points to enhance or fuel certain Monk features. You start knowing three such features: Flurry of Blows, Patient Defense, and Step of the Wind, each of which is detailed below.

When you expend a Focus Point, it is unavailable until you finish a Short or Long Rest, at the end of which you regain all your expended points.

Some features that use Focus Points require your target to make a saving throw. The save DC equals 8 plus your Wisdom modifier and Proficiency Bonus.

Flurry of Blows. You can expend 1 Focus Point to make two Unarmed Strikes as a Bonus Action.

Patient Defense. You can take the Disengage action as a Bonus Action. Alternatively, you can expend 1 Focus Point to take both the Disengage and the Dodge actions as a Bonus Action.

Step of the Wind. You can take the Dash action as a Bonus Action. Alternatively, you can expend 1 Focus Point to take both the Disengage and Dash actions as a Bonus Action, and your jump distance is doubled for the turn.

classFeatures.name: Unarmored Movement
classFeatures.description: Your speed increases by 10 feet while you aren’t wearing armor or wielding a Shield. This bonus increases when you reach certain Monk levels, as shown on the Monk Features table.

classFeatures.name: Uncanny Metabolism
classFeatures.description:
When you roll Initiative, you can regain all expended Focus Points. When you do so, roll your Martial Arts die, and regain a number of Hit Points equal to your Monk level plus the number rolled.

Once you use this feature, you can’t use it again until you finish a Long Rest.

classFeatures.name: Deflect Attacks
classFeatures.description:
When an attack roll hits you and its damage includes Bludgeoning, Piercing, or Slashing damage, you can take a Reaction to reduce the attack’s total damage against you. The reduction equals 1d10 plus your Dexterity modifier and Monk level.

If you reduce the damage to 0, you can expend 1 Focus Point to redirect some of the attack’s force. If you do so, choose a creature you can see within 5 feet of yourself if the attack was a melee attack or a creature you can see within 60 feet of yourself that isn’t behind Total Cover if the attack was a ranged attack. That creature must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw or take damage equal to two rolls of your Martial Arts die plus your Dexterity modifier. The damage is the same type dealt by the attack.

classFeatures.name: Monk Subclass
classFeatures.description: You gain a Monk subclass of your choice. A subclass is a specialization that grants you features at certain Monk levels. For the rest of your career, you gain each of your subclass’s features that are of your Monk level or lower.

classFeatures.name: Ability Score Improvement
classFeatures.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify. You gain this feature again at Monk levels 8, 12, and 16.

classFeatures.name: Slow Fall
classFeatures.description: You can take a Reaction when you fall to reduce any damage you take from the fall by an amount equal to five times your Monk level.

classFeatures.name: Extra Attack
classFeatures.description: You can attack twice instead of once whenever you take the Attack action on your turn.

classFeatures.name: Stunning Strike
classFeatures.description: Once per turn when you hit a creature with a Monk weapon or an Unarmed Strike, you can expend 1 Focus Point to attempt a stunning strike. The target must make a Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, the target has the Stunned condition until the start of your next turn. On a successful save, the target’s Speed is halved until the start of your next turn, and the next attack roll made against the target before then has Advantage.

classFeatures.name: Empowered Strikes
classFeatures.description: Whenever you deal damage with your Unarmed Strike, it can deal your choice of Force damage or its normal damage type.

classFeatures.name: Evasion
classFeatures.description:
When you’re subjected to an effect that allows you to make a Dexterity saving throw to take only half damage, you instead take no damage if you succeed on the saving throw and only half damage if you fail.

You don’t benefit from this feature if you have the Incapacitated condition.

classFeatures.name: 8: Ability Score Improvement
classFeatures.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify. You gain this feature again at Monk levels 12 and 16.

classFeatures.name: Acrobatic Movement
classFeatures.description: While you aren’t wearing armor or wielding a Shield, you gain the ability to move along vertical surfaces and across liquids on your turn without falling during the movement.

classFeatures.name: Heightened Focus
classFeatures.description:
Your Flurry of Blows, Patient Defense, and Step of the Wind gain the following benefits.

Flurry of Blows. You can expend 1 Focus Point to use Flurry of Blows and make three Unarmed Strikes with it instead of two.

Patient Defense. When you expend a Focus Point to use Patient Defense, you gain a number of Temporary Hit Points equal to two rolls of your Martial Arts die.

Step of the Wind. When you expend a Focus Point to use Step of the Wind, you can choose a willing creature within 5 feet of yourself that is Large or smaller. You move the creature with you until the end of your turn. The creature’s movement doesn’t provoke Opportunity Attacks.

classFeatures.name: Self-Restoration
classFeatures.description:
Through sheer force of will, you can remove one of the following conditions from yourself at the end of each of your turns: Charmed, Frightened, or Poisoned.

In addition, forgoing food and drink doesn’t give you levels of Exhaustion.

classFeatures.name: 12: Ability Score Improvement
classFeatures.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify. You gain this feature again at Monk level 16.

classFeatures.name: Deflect Energy
classFeatures.description: You can now use your Deflect Attacks feature against attacks that deal any damage type, not just Bludgeoning, Piercing, or Slashing.

classFeatures.name: Disciplined Survivor
classFeatures.description:
Your physical and mental discipline grant you proficiency in all saving throws.

Additionally, whenever you make a saving throw and fail, you can expend 1 Focus Point to reroll it, and you must use the new roll.

classFeatures.name: Perfect Focus
classFeatures.description: When you roll Initiative and don’t use Uncanny Metabolism, you regain expended Focus Points until you have 4 if you have 3 or fewer.

classFeatures.name: 16: Ability Score Improvement
classFeatures.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

classFeatures.name: Superior Defense
classFeatures.description: At the start of your turn, you can expend 3 Focus Points to bolster yourself against harm for 1 minute or until you have the Incapacitated condition. During that time, you have Resistance to all damage except Force damage.

classFeatures.name: Epic Boon
classFeatures.description: You gain an Epic Boon feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

classFeatures.name: Body and Mind
classFeatures.description: You have developed your body and mind to new heights. Your Dexterity and Wisdom scores increase by 4, to a maximum of 25.

definition.tagline: A Martial Artist of Supernatural Focus
definition.cardDescription: Monks use training and discipline to focus their internal power, channeling it to strike fast and hit hard.
definition.highlights:
• Focus your potential to create extraordinary, even supernatural, effects.
  • Channel uncanny speed to sidestep danger and strike like the wind.
  • Turn yourself into a living weapon.
definition.iconicGear:
• Shortswords, Spears, or Quarterstaffs
  • Loose, simple clothes
  • Wraps of Unarmed Power
definition.subclassFlavorText: At later levels you’ll become a Warrior.

subclassDefinition.name: Warrior of the Open Hand
subclassDefinition.description:
Master Unarmed Combat Techniques

Warriors of the Open Hand are masters of unarmed combat. They learn techniques to push and trip their opponents and manipulate their own energy to protect themselves from harm.

classFeatures.name: Open Hand Technique
classFeatures.description:
Whenever you hit a creature with an attack granted by your Flurry of Blows, you can impose one of the following effects on that target.

Addle. The target can’t make Opportunity Attacks until the start of its next turn.

Push. The target must succeed on a Strength saving throw or be pushed up to 15 feet away from you.

Topple. The target must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw or have the Prone condition.

classFeatures.name: Wholeness of Body
classFeatures.description:
You gain the ability to heal yourself. As a Bonus Action, you can roll your Martial Arts die. You regain a number of Hit Points equal to the number rolled plus your Wisdom modifier (minimum of 1 Hit Point regained).

You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum of once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a Long Rest.

classFeatures.name: Fleet Step
classFeatures.description: When you take a Bonus Action other than Step of the Wind, you can also use Step of the Wind immediately after that Bonus Action.

classFeatures.name: Quivering Palm
classFeatures.description:
You gain the ability to set up lethal vibrations in someone’s body. When you hit a creature with an Unarmed Strike, you can expend 4 Focus Points to start these imperceptible vibrations, which last for a number of days equal to your Monk level. The vibrations are harmless unless you take an action to end them. Alternatively, when you take the Attack action on your turn, you can forgo one of the attacks to end the vibrations. To end them, you and the target must be on the same plane of existence. When you end them, the target must make a Constitution saving throw, taking 10d12 Force damage on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one.

You can have only one creature under the effect of this feature at a time. You can end the vibrations harmlessly (no action required).

classFeatures.name: Core Monk Traits
classFeatures.description:
As a Level 1 Character:

  • Gain all the traits in the Core Monk Traits table.
  • Gain the Monk’s level 1 features.
Core Monk Traits
Primary Ability Dexterity and Wisdom
Hit Point Die D8 per Monk level
Saving Throw Proficiencies Strength and Dexterity
Skill Proficiencies Choose 2: Acrobatics, Athletics, History, Insight, Religion, or Stealth
Weapon Proficiencies Simple weapons and Martial weapons that have the Light property
Tool Proficiencies Choose one type of Artisan’s Tools or Musical Instrument
Armor Training None
Starting Equipment Choose A or B: (A) Spear, 5 Daggers, Artisan’s Tools or Musical Instrument chosen for the tool proficiency above, Explorer’s Pack, and 11 GP; or (B) 50 GP

classFeatures.description:

  • Simple Melee weapons
  • Martial Melee weapons that have the Light property

You gain the following benefits while you are unarmed or wielding only Monk weapons and you aren’t wearing armor or wielding a Shield.

Bonus Unarmed Strike. You can make an Unarmed Strike as a Bonus Action.

Martial Arts Die. You can roll 1d6 in place of the normal damage of your Unarmed Strike or Monk weapons. This die changes as you gain Monk levels, as shown in the Martial Arts column of the Monk Features table.

Dexterous Attacks. You can use your Dexterity modifier instead of your Strength modifier for the attack and damage rolls of your Unarmed Strikes and Monk weapons. In addition, when you use the Grapple or Shove option of your Unarmed Strike, you can use your Dexterity modifier instead of your Strength modifier to determine the save DC.
classFeatures.summary: Apply combat styles that use your Unarmed Strike and certain weapons.

definition.snippet: When subjected to an effect that allows you to make a Dex. saving throw to take only half damage, you instead take no damage if you succeed, and only half damage if you fail. You don’t benefit from this feature if you have the Incapacitated condition.

definition.name: 8: Ability Score Improvement
definition.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify. You gain this feature again at Monk levels 12 and 16.

definition.name: Acrobatic Movement
definition.description: While you aren’t wearing armor or wielding a Shield, you gain the ability to move along vertical surfaces and across liquids on your turn without falling during the movement.
definition.snippet: While you aren’t wearing armor or wielding a Shield, you gain the ability to move along vertical surfaces and across liquids on your turn without falling during the movement.

definition.name: Heightened Focus
definition.description:
Your Flurry of Blows, Patient Defense, and Step of the Wind gain the following benefits.

Flurry of Blows. You can expend 1 Focus Point to use Flurry of Blows and make three Unarmed Strikes with it instead of two.

Patient Defense. When you expend a Focus Point to use Patient Defense, you gain a number of Temporary Hit Points equal to two rolls of your Martial Arts die.

Step of the Wind. When you expend a Focus Point to use Step of the Wind, you can choose a willing creature within 5 feet of yourself that is Large or smaller. You move the creature with you until the end of your turn. The creature’s movement doesn’t provoke Opportunity Attacks.
definition.snippet: Your Flurry of Blows, Patient Defense, and Step of the Wind gain the following benefits. Flurry of Blows. You can expend 1 Focus Point to use Flurry of Blows and make 3 Unarmed Strikes instead of two. Patient Defense. When you expend a Focus Point to use Patient Defense, you gain {{scalevalue}} Temp HP. Step of the Wind. When you expend a Focus Point to use Step of the Wind, you can choose a willing creature within 5 ft. that is Large or smaller. You move the creature with you until the end of your turn without provoking Opportunity Attacks.

definition.name: Self-Restoration
definition.description:
Through sheer force of will, you can remove one of the following conditions from yourself at the end of each of your turns: Charmed, Frightened, or Poisoned.

In addition, forgoing food and drink doesn’t give you levels of Exhaustion.
definition.snippet:
You can remove one of the following conditions from yourself at the end of each of your turns: Charmed, Frightened, or Poisoned. 

In addition, forgoing food and drink doesn’t give you levels of Exhaustion.

definition.name: 12: Ability Score Improvement
definition.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify. You gain this feature again at Monk level 16.

definition.name: Deflect Energy
definition.description: You can now use your Deflect Attacks feature against attacks that deal any damage type, not just Bludgeoning, Piercing, or Slashing.
definition.snippet: You can now use your Deflect Attacks feature against attacks that deal any damage type, not just Bludgeoning, Piercing, or Slashing.

definition.name: Disciplined Survivor
definition.description:
Your physical and mental discipline grant you proficiency in all saving throws.

Additionally, whenever you make a saving throw and fail, you can expend 1 Focus Point to reroll it, and you must use the new roll.
definition.snippet:
You have proficiency in all saving throws.

Additionally, whenever you fail a saving throw, you can expend 1 Focus Point to reroll it, and you must use the new roll.

definition.name: Perfect Focus
definition.description: When you roll Initiative and don’t use Uncanny Metabolism, you regain expended Focus Points until you have 4 if you have 3 or fewer.
definition.snippet: When you roll Initiative with 3 or fewer Focus Points and don’t use Uncanny Metabolism, you regain expended points until you have at least 4.

definition.name: 16: Ability Score Improvement
definition.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

definition.name: Superior Defense
definition.description: At the start of your turn, you can expend 3 Focus Points to bolster yourself against harm for 1 minute or until you have the Incapacitated condition. During that time, you have Resistance to all damage except Force damage.
definition.snippet: At the start of your turn, you can expend 3 Focus Points to have Resistance to all damage except Force damage for 1 minute or until you have the Incapacitated condition.

definition.name: Epic Boon
definition.description: You gain an Epic Boon feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

definition.name: Body and Mind
definition.description: You have developed your body and mind to new heights. Your Dexterity and Wisdom scores increase by 4, to a maximum of 25.
definition.snippet: Your Dex. and Wis. scores increase by 4, to a maximum of 25.

definition.name: Tavern Brawler
definition.description:
Origin Feat

You gain the following benefits.

Enhanced Unarmed Strike. When you hit with your Unarmed Strike and deal damage, you can deal Bludgeoning damage equal to 1d4 plus your Strength modifier instead of the normal damage of an Unarmed Strike.

Damage Rerolls. Whenever you roll a damage die for your Unarmed Strike, you can reroll the die if it rolls a 1, and you must use the new roll.

Improvised Weaponry. You have proficiency with improvised weapons.

Push. When you hit a creature with an Unarmed Strike as part of the Attack action on your turn, you can deal damage to the target and also push it 5 feet away from you. You can use this benefit only once per turn.
definition.snippet: Enhanced Unarmed Strike. When you hit a creature with an Unarmed Strike, you can deal damage to the target and also push it 5 ft. away from you. You can use this benefit only once per turn.

definition.name: Dark Bargain
definition.description: At your GM's discretion, you can take a Dark Bargain.
definition.snippet: At your GM's discretion, you have taken a Dark Bargain.

definition.name: Zhentarim Mercenary Ability Score Increase
definition.description: The Zhentarim Mercenary Background allows you to choose between Strength, Dexterity, and Charisma. Increase one of these scores by 2 and another one by 1, or increase all three by 1. None of these increases can raise a score above 20.

definition.name: Zhentarim Ruffian
definition.description:
Origin Feat

You gain the following benefits.

Exploit Opening. When you roll damage for an Opportunity Attack, you can roll the damage dice twice and use either roll against the target.

Family First. If you have Heroic Inspiration when you roll Initiative, you can expend it to give yourself and your allies Advantage on that Initiative roll.

Family First. If you have Heroic Inspiration when you roll Initiative, you can expend it to give yourself and your allies Advantage on that Initiative roll.

definition.name: Character Threads
definition.description: Threads are aspects added to characters at any time during their career that have a specific short or long-term goal.
definition.snippet: Threads are aspects added to characters at any time during their career that have a specific short or long-term goal.

definition.name: Runestones
definition.description: At the beginning of the Northlands Sagas adventure path, each player should draw one Runestone randomly.
definition.snippet: At the beginning of the Northlands Sagas adventure path, each player should draw one Runestone randomly.

definition.name: Echoing Soul
definition.description:
Dark Gift Feat (Prerequisite: Ravenloft Campaign)

You experience echoes from a past or alternate life. You gain the following features.

Channeled Prowess. You have proficiency in two skills of your choice.

In addition, choose one skill you have proficiency in. You gain Expertise in that skill. Whenever you finish a Long Rest, you can change your choice for this benefit.

Inherent Tongues. You know one additional language of your choice, which you choose from the language tables in the Player’s Handbook.

Intrusive Echoes. Immediately after you make a D20 Test and roll a 1 on the d20, memories and sensations from your soul’s other life threaten to overtake you. Make a Constitution saving throw (DC 13 plus your Proficiency Bonus). On a failed save, you have the Incapacitated condition until the end of your next turn. While you are Incapacitated in this way, your Speed is halved.

Immediately after you make a D20 Test and roll a 1 on the d20, make a Con. saving throw (DC {{13+proficiency#unsigned}}). On a failed save, you have the Incapacitated condition until the end of your next turn. While Incapacitated in this way, your Speed is halved.

definition.name: Shape-Shift
definition.description:
As an action, you can shape-shift to change your appearance and your voice. You determine the specifics of the changes, including your coloration, hair length, and sex. You can also adjust your height and weight and can change your size between Medium and Small. You can make yourself appear as a member of another playable species, though none of your game statistics change. You can’t duplicate the appearance of an individual you’ve never seen, and you must adopt a form that has the same basic arrangement of limbs that you have. This trait doesn’t change your clothing and equipment.

While shape-shifted with this trait, you have Advantage on Charisma checks.

You stay in the new form until you take an action to revert to your true form.

This trait doesn’t change your clothing and equipment.

While shape-shifted with this trait, you have Advantage on Cha. checks.

You stay in the new form until you take an action to revert to your true form.

definition.name: Increase two scores (+2 / +1)
definition.description: Increase one of these scores by 2 and a different score by 1.

class.label: Choose a Musical Instrument or Artisan's Tools

feat.label: Choose +2 to Strength, Dexterity, or Charisma
Choose +1 to Strength, Dexterity, or Charisma
Select a Standard or Rare Language

options.label: Bestial Awakening (Intelligence)
options.description:
The feral world calls to your soul, howling for you to join it. You can give in to the call and transform into a beast, but the touch of silver bites you more deeply than the sharpest fang.

Beast Skin. You always have the Polymorph spell prepared. You can cast it once without a spell slot or spell components but can target only yourself, and you regain the ability to cast it in that way when you finish a Long Rest. You can also cast it using slots you have of the appropriate level. Intelligence is your spellcasting ability for the spell when you cast it without a spell slot.

Searing Silver. Damage from Silvered Weapons ignores any Resistance or Immunity to damage you have. If you take damage from a Silvered Weapon or touch silver with your bare skin (or its equivalent), you can’t regain Hit Points until you finish a Short or Long Rest.

options.label: Call of the Lonely
options.description:
In the depths of your solitude, and when your hope withered on the branch, you were granted a companion you can call on. You never have to be alone again. This companion is Friendly toward you but is deeply unsettling to your allies.

Never Alone. When no other creature can see you, you can use a Bonus Action to call your companion, which appears in an unoccupied space within 10 feet of you. It takes the form of a person or a vague silhouette, and it uses the Doppelganger stat block. It is Friendly toward you and acts as your ally, and it takes its turn on your Initiative in combat. It disappears after 1 hour, when it dies, when you die, or when you use an action to politely dismiss it. When it disappears, it drops anything it was wearing or carrying.

Once you use this Bonus Action, you can’t use it again until you finish a Long Rest.

Precocious Friend. The companion sometimes appears with trinkets, Cursed Curios*, or other items. It might tell you where it got them, or it might not (GM’s choice). If it took the object from someone who misses it or wants it back, that could create difficulties for you.

Unsettling Presence. While your companion is summoned, both it and you exude a frightful air that unnerves your allies. While the companion is summoned, a creature that isn’t Hostile toward you that starts its turn able to see you or the companion must succeed on a DC 15 Wisdom saving throw or have the Frightened condition until the start of its next turn.

options.label: Collector of Stories (Intelligence)
options.description:
You have an insatiable desire to gather the tales of the world. Sometimes, you inadvertently take the story of meeting another person and keep it to yourself, forever.

Gathered Tales. You always have the Legend Lore spell prepared. You can cast it once without a spell slot or spell components, and you regain the ability to cast it in that way when you finish a Long Rest. You can also cast it using slots you have of the appropriate level. Intelligence is your spellcasting ability for the spell when you cast it without a spell slot.

Hoarded Stories. When you use this bargain to cast Legend Lore without a spell slot, a Friendly NPC (the GM chooses) forgets ever meeting you. You can meet them again and attempt to rebuild a relationship, but you forever cause an unsettling discomfort in them. That NPC’s attitude toward you can’t ever improve beyond Indifferent, and you have Disadvantage on all Charisma checks against them.

options.label: Crooked Fortune
options.description:
The crooked fate that governs Druskenvald has taken a liking to you. You can avoid calamity, but you must pay back your good fortune eventually.

Cheat Fate. When you fail a saving throw, you can choose to succeed instead. When you do so, if you would take half damage on a successful save, you take no damage instead. Once you use Cheat Fate, you can’t use it again until you pay the price (see below).

Delayed Price. After you use Cheat Fate, your next three saving throws have Disadvantage and can’t have Advantage, and you gain 1 Exhaustion level when you succeed on one of the saves. Once you succeed or fail on the third saving throw, you regain the use of Cheat Fate. The powers that govern the warp and weft of fate don’t take kindly to cheating outside their terms—at the GM’s discretion, saving throws made intentionally or without true consequences simply to pay this price painlessly don’t count.

options.label: Cult of Personality
options.description:
You are a force of charm and allure with which to be reckoned. While others fall over themselves to stumble into your good graces, you might find that you end up needing them more than they need you.

Honeyed Words. As a Magic action, you can speak sweet words to a creature within 60 feet of you. The creature has the Charmed condition for 24 hours, until you use this feature again, or until you or your allies deal any damage to the target or force it to make a saving throw. The Charmed target is your ally and follows your commands as long as they don’t require the target to harm itself. If the target carries out a command it finds odious, it makes a DC 15 Wisdom saving throw, ending the condition on itself on a success. Once the condition ends, the target is immune to this feature for 24 hours.

If the target has Immunity to the Charmed condition, it instead has Disadvantage on D20 Tests until the start of your next turn, and you gain a number of Temporary Hit Points equal to your level. Once you use this feature, you can’t use it again until you finish a Short or Long Rest.

Solitary Weakness. If you start your turn with no allies that you can see or hear within 20 feet of you, you lose any Temporary Hit Points you have from any source, and you have Disadvantage on D20 Tests until the start of your next turn.

options.label: Hand of Death
options.description:
Death has no claim on you. In exchange for staying its cold hand without that hand closing, you become a maw through which death feeds on the world around you.

Adamant Endurance. You have Advantage on Constitution saving throws.

Cling to Life. When you have 0 Hit Points at the start of your turn, or when you die, you can instead choose to regain a number of Hit Points equal to half your maximum. Once you use this feature, you can’t use it again. After using it, whenever you regain Hit Points through any means, you gain only half that amount. Once a Humanoid (other than you) dies within 30 feet of you, you regain the use of this feature, and you can regain Hit Points as normal.

Death Knell. Other creatures within 10 feet of you have Disadvantage on Death Saving Throws, and you can’t restore Hit Points to a creature with 0 Hit Points by any means.

options.label: Might of the Old
options.description:
Ancient and primeval power dwells within the oldest forests of Druskenvald. These powers pour their strength into you but can overwhelm you with their unchanging nature.

Ancient Might. Your Strength score increases by 2, to a maximum of 22, and your Dexterity score decreases by 2.

Primeval Strength. As a Bonus Action, you can call upon the might of the ancients to raise your Strength score to 25 for 1 minute, during which time you deal double damage to objects. Once you use this Bonus Action, you can’t use it again until you finish a Long Rest.

Pyre’s Price. When you activate Primeval Strength, your skin takes on a bark-like texture, during which time you have Vulnerability to Fire damage. Your skin returns to normal when you finish a Long Rest.

options.label: Self-Serving Sacrifice (Intelligence)
options.description:
You can snatch others from the jaws of death, but your own grip lingers too tightly. The feeling of saving others from suffering fills you with resolve, even as it drains the vigor from those you rescue.

Critical Savior. When a creature within 120 feet of you is subjected to a Critical Hit or is reduced to 0 Hit Points, you can use your Reaction to magically switch places with the target. Each of you teleports to the other’s space. Roll a number of d6s equal to half your level. You take Force damage equal to the total rolled, you gain Heroic Inspiration, and your Exhaustion level, if any, is reduced by 1. The target regains Hit Points equal to the total rolled and gains 1 Exhaustion level. You can’t use this feature again until you finish a Short or Long Rest.

Draining Presence. When you finish a Short or Long Rest, each creature within 100 feet of you loses one Hit Point Die (before spending Hit Dice in the case of finishing a Short Rest).

Final Sacrifice. You can cast True Resurrection using this bargain requiring no spell slot or spell components. Intelligence is your spellcasting ability for the spell when you cast it without a spell slot.

When you cast the spell this way, you die, and nothing can prevent it. Your soul is bound to torment the creature you returned to life, and you can’t be raised from the dead by any means. When the creature takes a Long Rest, it must roll a d20 halfway through the rest’s duration. On a 1, your soul takes the form of a Wraith within 100 feet of the creature and tries to kill it. The wraith vanishes after 8 hours, or when it dies. Once the wraith appears, it can’t appear again for 30 days.

options.label: Shrouded Spirit
options.description:
The pall of death drapes over you like the Shroud of Druskenvald. This shroud protects you from the depredations of the Undead, but it draws you ever nearer to their cold embrace.

Inured to Undeath. You have Resistance to Necrotic damage, and you have Advantage on saving throws against spells and effects created by Undead.

Unholy Appetite. You must consume one pound of raw flesh (it needn’t be fresh) per day or suffer Malnutrition.

Withering Soul. Features that detect your creature type, such as a Paladin’s Divine Sense or the Detect Evil and Good spell, treat you as Undead if your creature type isn’t Undead already.

options.label: Skin of Coffin Nails
options.description:
Your body becomes as coldly unrelenting as iron nails sealing away the dead. You can shrug off staggering punishment, but the waters of the world no longer embrace you, as if spiting your defiance.

Iron Grit. You gain Resistance to Bludgeoning, Piercing, or Slashing damage (your choice when you select this Dark Bargain). You can change the damage type to one of the other options when you finish a Short or Long Rest. When you suffer a Critical Hit from the chosen damage type, it becomes a normal hit instead.

Watery Doom. When you are immersed in water or splashed with at least 1 gallon of water (such as from a bucket or being rained on), your skin tightens and grows rusty until you finish a Short or Long Rest. During that time, your Speed and the time you can hold your breath are halved, and your Resistance to the chosen damage type for Iron Grit becomes Vulnerability instead. At the GM’s discretion, other liquids can also trigger this feature.

options.label: Stilled Hungers
options.description:
Food, drink, and maladies of the flesh have lost their hold over you, but these are the very things that keep a soul vital while in the land of the living. Your mind grows ravenous absent those urges—your thoughts crack with gnawing fears, and your dreams wither into crooked nightmares.

Sated Cravings. Forgoing food and drink doesn’t give you Exhaustion levels. You have Resistance to Poison damage, and you have Advantage on saving throws to avoid or end the Poisoned condition.

Tenuous Psyche. You have Vulnerability to Psychic damage and Disadvantage on saving throws to avoid or end the Frightened condition. A Calm Emotions spell cast on you suppresses this feature for its duration.

options.label: Unquenchable Fury
options.description:
Anger is a powerful tool. This bargain stokes it into a formidable inferno, but it threatens to consume everything in its path.

Furious Onslaught. As a Bonus Action, or when you take the Attack action on your turn, you can choose to enter a fury. While in this fury, you can make one additional melee attack when you take the Attack action on your turn, and you reduce all damage you take by an amount equal to your Proficiency Bonus. The fury ends if you have the Incapacitated condition, if you die, or if you end your turn and there are no creatures within 60 feet of you that you can see or hear. Once you use this feature, you can’t use it again until you finish a Long Rest. You also regain the use of this feature when you kill a Humanoid.

Red Haze. While you are in a fury, you must make a DC 15 Wisdom saving throw at the start of each of your turns. On a successful save, you can choose to end your fury or continue it. On a failed save, you no longer make the saving throw at the start of your turns, and you regard the creature nearest to you that you can see or hear as your enemy (if there are multiple, choose one at random). On each of your turns, you must move as close to the creature as possible and take the Attack action, targeting that creature with melee attacks. If you’re unable to get close enough to the creature to attack it, your turn ends after you’ve used up all your available movement. If the creature dies or can no longer be seen or heard by you, the next nearest creature that you can see or hear becomes your new target.

options.label: Wings of Whispers
options.description:
You have been gifted with secrets that you can call upon in the form of ravens and crows to bear you aloft, but at the risk of revealing your own secrets in turn.

Murderous Flight. As a Bonus Action, you can magically gain a Fly Speed equal to your Speed (and you can hover) for 10 minutes. During this time, a mass of shadows takes the shape of a murder of crows and ravens (but they aren’t truly creatures). The birds appear in a 20-foot Emanation originating from you and take flight and whirl around you when you fly. Once you use this feature, you can’t do so again until you finish a Short or Long Rest, unless you gain 1 Exhaustion level to use it again early.

Whispered Secrets. The birds that appear when you activate Murderous Flight remain and continue to follow you until you finish a Short or Long Rest. While the birds are present, you have Disadvantage on Dexterity (Stealth) checks, and the birds whisper your secrets into the minds of other creatures, granting them Advantage on Wisdom (Insight or Perception) checks made against you.

options.label: Increase three scores (+1 / +1 / +1)
options.description: Increase your Strength, Dexterity, and Charisma by 1.

options.label: Increase two scores (+2 / +1)
options.description: Increase one of these scores by 2 and a different score by 1.

options.label: Bloodsworn (Level 1)
options.description:
Least Milestone Feature

When you achieve the first milestone of this thread, you gain your choice of one of the following benefits.

Cunning. You gain proficiency in your choice of one of the following skills: Deception, Insight, or Intimidation.

Tool of Vengeance. You gain a Common magic item of your choice.

You have been wronged. Now your fate is painted with blood.

Among the Northlanders, no oath is more serious than an oath of vengeance. But when justice cannot be achieved by any other means, a sworn promise to mete out revenge for wrongdoing is the only answer that remains. Those who make such oaths are called Bloodsworn, and their vengeance becomes a tale told by skalds for generations.

As a Bloodsworn, your ultimate goal is revenge upon the one (or ones) who wronged you. Unfortunately, the wrongdoer is beyond your reach, at least for now. This means your plan must be carried out over weeks, months, or even longer. During this time, you’ll no doubt be distracted by other adventures, but your focus demands that you always return to your oath. Bloodsworn must have been wronged or harmed in some way before the gods will accept their blood oath. In most cases, your vengeance will be fatal to your target, but in some situations, other punishments may be more fitting, especially if the wrong you’re avenging doesn’t itself involve a death.

Goals. Work with your GM to create a goal specific to your campaign or use one from the Bloodsworn Goals table. The specific nature of each milestone is dependent on the goal selected and should be agreed upon by both player and GM. Use the suggested milestone benefits described in this section or create new ones based on the rules for creating a thread.

Bloodsworn Goals
d6 Goal
1 I must kill the one who murdered the one I love.
2 I cannot rest until the usurper who killed my jarl is dead by my hands.
3 A crew of scheming charlatans ruined my family’s business. I vow to punish them in ways the law cannot.
4 The power-mad jarl of our lands has destroyed my community. I have a cunning plan to ensure he is overthrown.
5 My trusted shieldmate betrayed me and left me to die. Now I shall return the favor.
6 I was shamed by the false words of another. I will deliver vengeance tenfold upon my accuser.

options.label: Bloodsworn (Level 5)
options.description:
Lesser Milestone Feature

When you achieve this level of milestone, you gain your choice of one of the following benefits.

Enemy of My Enemy. You’ve made an ally, a person who is also sworn to oppose your enemy. They have granted you an underground hideout from which to conduct your campaign of vengeance.

Wrath. You can cast Wrathful Smite, using your choice of INT, WIS, or CHA as your spellcasting ability score. Once you use this feature, you can’t use it again until you finish a Long Rest.

Cunning. You gain proficiency in your choice of one of the following skills: Deception, Insight, or Intimidation.

Tenacity. You can resist effects that would slow your oath from being carried out. options.label: Cursemarked (Level 1)
options.description:
Least Milestone Feature

When you achieve the first milestone of this thread, you gain the following benefits.

Tides of Fate [1–3, Saving Throws]. Your Speed is reduced by 10 feet. When this occurs, the Speed of another creature of your choice within 30 feet of you increases by 10 feet. These effects last until the end of your next turn.

Greater Sacrifice. When your Tides of Fate is triggered, you can choose to reduce your Speed to 0 until the start of your next turn. If you do so, in addition to the normal benefit, the chosen creature does not provoke opportunity attacks and ignores Difficult Terrain until the end of your next turn. Once you use your Greater Sacrifice feature, you can’t use it again until you finish a Long Rest.

You bear the brunt of a curse of ill luck, and it’ll get worse before the end.

Northlanders place great faith in the existence of luck. The gods can bestow good fortune upon the faithful, and honorable actions can directly lead to successful endeavors. Likewise, disreputable behavior can bring failure not only to you, but to future generations of your family.

You are one of the unfortunates, chosen by fate or the gods to suffer from ill luck wherever you go. Perhaps you earned this curse through dishonor, oathbreaking, or cowardice. You might bear misfortune as a virtual heirloom, passed down to you by a disreputable ancestor. Other cursemarked are victims of witch’s curses or similar supernatural effects. Whatever the reason, you can’t seem to catch a break.

Strangely, the presence of a Cursemarked can often bring good fortune to those nearby. As long as your friends are willing to put up with your occasional mishaps, the long-term benefit might well be worthwhile. Meanwhile, however, the cursemarked searches for some way to atone for their misdeeds, real or imagined, knowing such a road will be long and will certainly grow worse before the end. Yet when the hand of fortune is turned against you, what else can you do?

Goals. Work with your GM to create a goal specific to your campaign or use one from the Cursemarked Goals table. The specific nature of each milestone is dependent on the goal selected and should be agreed upon by both player and GM. Use the suggested milestone benefits described in this section or create new ones based on the rules for creating a thread.

Cursemarked Goals
d6 Goal
1 I will atone for my dishonor by returning a sacred relic to a distant tomb.
2 I can only lift this curse by clearing the name of an ancestor falsely accused of murder.
3 I must prove my courage to the gods by defeating a tribe of marauding giants.
4 I will invoke Freyr’s blessing by forging peace between two warring clans.
5 To end this curse, I shall endure trials of my honor, my valor, and my selflessness.
6 I must convince Wotan himself to intervene and convince the Norns to reshape my fate.
Cursemarked Milestone Rules

Cursemarked milestones work a bit differently than those of other character threads. The milestone features described below trigger when you roll for specific mechanics (saving throws, ability checks or attack roll, depending on the feature) and get a result within the range of numbers indicated in the bracket for each milestone. Milestone features never overlap, so if you trigger a feature, you can’t trigger another until the start of your next turn after the duration of the previous one expires.

options.label: Cursemarked (Level 5)
options.description:
Lesser Milestone Feature

When you achieve this level of milestone, you lose all prior milestone feature benefits from this thread and gain the following benefits.

Burden’s Shield [1–5, Saving Throws, Ability Checks]. Your AC is reduced by 2. When this occurs, the AC of another creature of your choice within 30 feet of you increases by 2. These effects last until the end of your next turn.

Greater Sacrifice. When your Burden’s Shield is triggered, you can choose to reduce your AC by 4 instead of 2. If you do so, the AC of the chosen creature increases by 4 (instead of 2), or you can choose for it to receive the benefit of the Tide of Fate’s Greater Sacrifice. Once you use your Greater Sacrifice feature, you can’t use it again until you finish a Long Rest.

options.description:

options.label: Explorer (Level 1)
options.description:
Least Milestone Feature

When you achieve the first milestone of this thread, you gain your choice of one of the following benefits.

Celebrity Explorer. You receive a 30% discount on lodging, supplies, and food from innkeepers, merchants, and proprietors, and it’s common for them to offer you things for free.

Explorer’s Aptitude. Your travels instill you with new knowledge. You gain proficiency in your choice of one of the following skills: Athletics, History, or Perception.

Glory lies just over the horizon. Time to find it.

Explorers occupy an unusual place in Northlands society. Though revered for their courage, they rarely find a comfortable place in a community. Driven by wanderlust, curiosity, boredom, or a simple inability to relate to civilized folks, the Explorer spends most of their time in the wilderness. Some climb mountains, others set sail for lost lands, and still others delve through dark tunnels beneath the earth. For an Explorer, the greatest treasure awaits beyond the next hill.

Whether by choice or by fate, you follow the path less traveled. Perhaps you’re a rugged seafarer or mountaineer seeking discoveries in the wild. Other Explorers are treasure hunters, royal mapmakers, or seekers of truth. You might be an exile, outlaw, or iconoclast, who simply can’t get along with others. Or you might be driven by vision or prophecy to forsake hearth and home for the unknown.

 Work with your GM to create a goal specific to your campaign or use one from the Explorer Goals table. The specific nature of each milestone is dependent on the goal selected and should be agreed upon by both player and GM. Use the suggested milestone benefits described in this section or create new ones based on the rules for creating a thread.

GM Note. This thread brings flavorful Northlands themes into a campaign and can be taken up by characters of any class. However, it can tread on the toes of any Rangers in the party. Consult with your Ranger players before allowing a character to take on this thread to make sure no feelings will be hurt.

Explorer Goals
d6 Goal
1 I shall find a route through the Bloodfjord Mountains never before traveled by mortals.
2 To avert Ragnarok, I must carry a prophecy to a far-flung land.
3 My mother died searching for the headwaters of the Tearstain River. I shall complete her quest.
4 I seek a sapling of the World Tree, rumored to be growing on a tiny island in the Nieder Strait.
5 Once I decipher the ancient map I hold, I’ll delve into the depths of Narvegr Maw.
6 The Frozen Palace of the last Hyperborean lies somewhere in the Bleak Expanse. I’ll find it and return with his icy crown.

options.label: Explorer (Level 5)
options.description:
Lesser Milestone Feature

When you achieve this level of milestone, you gain your choice of one of the following benefits.

Traversal Expert. You have a knack for finding easy paths through the environment. You may use your Bonus Action to move your Speed. During this movement, you ignore Difficult Terrain and have Advantage on Strength (Athletics) checks to climb, jump, or swim. Once you use this feature, you can’t use it again until you finish a Short or Long Rest.

Scout’s Awareness. You have a preternatural sense that allows you to avoid dangers in your surroundings. For 1 hour, you and each ally within 30 feet who can see you have Advantage on saving throws to resist the effects of nonmagical traps and hazards. Once you use this feature, you can’t use it again until you finish a Long Rest.

Least Milestone Feature

When you achieve the first milestone of this thread, you gain your choice of one of the following benefits.

Celebrity Explorer. You receive a 30% discount on lodging, supplies, and food from innkeepers, merchants, and proprietors, and it’s common for them to offer you things for free.

Explorer’s Aptitude. Your travels instill you with new knowledge. You gain proficiency in your choice of one of the following skills: Athletics, History, or Perception.

options.label: Explorer (Level 11)
options.description:
Greater Milestone Feature

When you achieve this level of milestone, you gain one of the following benefits.

Ennobled. A monarch has recognized your achievements as an Explorer in their or other territories by granting you a noble title, which comes with a moderately sized estate. You have Advantage on Charisma checks with anyone who would be impressed or intimidated by your title. You also can avoid punishment for minor infractions, though if you abuse this, your title might be revoked.

Wayfarer’s Steps. You can never become lost by nonmagical means and can always retrace your steps. Additionally, as a Bonus Action, you can take the Dash action and command up to 4 willing creatures who can hear you to use their Reaction to immediately take a Dash action. Neither you nor your allies trigger Opportunity Attacks while moving in this way. Once you use this Bonus Action, you can’t use it again until you finish a Long Rest.

options.description:

When you achieve this level of milestone, you gain the following benefits.

Far Traveler. While traveling, you and your companions may travel at a Fast Pace without any penalties. You always find good campsites and have Advantage on any d20 rolls related to camping or foraging in the wilderness. You also have Advantage on Animal Handling checks to calm wild animals.

One With the Land. You may study the land from a good vantage point (such as a high position or advantageous overlook) for 1 minute and ask a question concerning the natural world or locations within 5 miles. This functions like the Commune with Nature spell. Once you use this feature, you must wait 24 hours before using it again.

options.label: Fatebound (Level 1)
options.description:
Least Milestone Feature

When you achieve the first milestone of this thread, you gain your choice of one the following benefits.

Dream Gift. A god, Norn, or other powerful being has given you a gift in a dream. When you awoke, you had a Common magic item of the GM’s choice that may aid you.

Fate’s Blessing. Once per day, while you are Bloodied, you can use your Reaction to get Advantage on a saving throw.

Your fate is written. Make it glorious.

Many warriors loudly boast of their desire for a noble death or an honorable end in battle, to thus earn a seat in the great hall of Valhalla. These boasts are but hopeful wishes since most individuals never see the path of their future. You, however, are not most individuals. You have been blessed, or perhaps cursed, with the knowledge of your end, though this knowledge doesn’t tell you if you succeed or fail in the circumstance surrounding your death. You might know you will die facing a dark warlord, but you don’t know if you will defeat her. Despite this, the Norns have given you a rare opportunity. The knowledge you possess will help you ensure your death is worthy of the sagas.

Some of those who are Fatebound are marked from birth. Others learn of their fate from seers, shamans, or divine visions. Some heroes earn this thread after engaging in activities that move the gods or the Norns to reward them with such a potent end.

Fatebound and Death. Keep in mind that having this thread may mean you can’t die before your fated time, but it doesn’t mean you will reach that point whole and healthy. While your fate is inevitable, luck alone determines your condition when you meet that end. Do you lead a glorious charge across the battlefield? Or are you left guarding the baggage? Has every confrontation leading up to your end prepared you to defeat your ultimate foe? Or has a lifetime of avoidance left you ill-prepared?

If a Fatebound PC is killed before their time, and magical resurrection is not an option, a GM could instead choose for them to be injured in a significant way—such as the loss of a limb or eye. These injuries should not be able to be healed without high-level magic. Alternatively, perhaps they lose one of their powerful magical items instead of their lives, or they might suffer a level of Exhaustion until they reach their next milestone. The GM and player should agree on this complication.

Goals. Work with your GM to create a goal specific to your campaign or use one from the Fatebound Goals table. The specific nature of each milestone is dependent on the goal selected and should be agreed upon by both player and GM. Use the suggested milestone benefits described in this section or create new ones based on the rules for creating a thread.

GM Note: The Fatebound is an unusual thread in that its final milestone specifically describes the manner in which a character might meet with a permanent death, likely as part of the final accomplishment of their goal, and then empowers the PCs’ allies as a reward. Most importantly, this thread should be something the player consciously selects.

Fatebound Goals
d6 Goal
1 I will die only after finding and facing the mightiest dragon in the land.
2 I will be slain by Utgard-Loki while attempting to thwart his plan to slay the gods.
3 To defend my people from invasion, I pledge to pay any price, including my own life.
4 I shall search for and find a fabled lost treasure, but I will die in the process.
5 I will rise to become the North’s greatest reaver, then perish battling the World Serpent.
6 I seek the truth behind the fall of Nordheim, though it will cost my life to learn it.

options.description:

When you achieve this level of milestone, you gain your choice of one of the following benefits.

Strength of Wyrd. While you are Bloodied, once per turn you may add your Proficiency Bonus to a damage roll. You may use this damage bonus a number of times equal to your Proficiency Bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a Long Rest.

Enduring Wyrd. When you take damage, you may use your Reaction to gain Temporary Hit Points equal to your Proficiency Bonus. You cannot use this feature again until you complete a Short or Long Rest.

Knight of Fate. You have become a legend in a territory, and the monarch there has knighted you, granting you holdings that will revert back to the monarchy upon your death. Dream Gift. A god, Norn, or other powerful being has given you a gift in a dream. When you awoke, you had a Common magic item of the GM’s choice that may aid you.

Fate’s Blessing. Once per day, while you are Bloodied, you can use your Reaction to get Advantage on a saving throw.

options.label: Fatebound (Level 17)
options.description:
Superior Milestone Feature

When you achieve this level of milestone, you gain the following benefits.

Last Act of Fate. At a moment you have predetermined to be the time when you are to die, when you are reduced 0 Hit Points or killed outright, you may (no action required) instead set your Hit Points to 1. You lose any detrimental conditions, and any harmful effects on you are ended immediately. Until the end of your next turn, you gain Advantage on all attack rolls and ability checks, you have Immunity to all damage and conditions (and other effects at the GM’s discretion), and you add your character level to all damage rolls.

At the end of your next turn, you die and can never be restored to life.

Glorious End. If you achieve your destiny in a way that earns you a seat in Valhalla (or some other appropriate afterlife), the allies who witness your death have Advantage on all d20 rolls for 24 hours as they are inspired by your heroism.

Greater Milestone Feature

When you achieve this level of milestone, you gain the following benefit.

Doom Delayed. When you would be killed, you may instead declare yourself as Stable with 0 Hit Points. Once you use this feature, you can’t use it again until you finish a Long Rest.

Knight of Fate. You have become a legend in a territory, and the monarch there has knighted you, granting you holdings that will revert back to the monarchy upon your death. You have Advantage on Charisma-based checks with the people of this land and are frequently showered with minor gifts and favors there.

Lesser Milestone Feature

When you achieve this level of milestone, you gain your choice of one of the following benefits.

Strength of Wyrd. While you are Bloodied, once per turn you may add your Proficiency Bonus to a damage roll. You may use this damage bonus a number of times equal to your Proficiency Bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a Long Rest.

Enduring Wyrd. When you take damage, you may use your Reaction to gain Temporary Hit Points equal to your Proficiency Bonus. You cannot use this feature again until you complete a Short or Long Rest.

Least Milestone Feature

When you achieve the first milestone of this thread, you gain your choice of one the following benefits.

Dream Gift. A god, Norn, or other powerful being has given you a gift in a dream. When you awoke, you had a Common magic item of the GM’s choice that may aid you.

Fate’s Blessing. Once per day, while you are Bloodied, you can use your Reaction to get Advantage on a saving throw.

options.label: Herald (Level 1)
options.description:
Least Milestone Feature

When you achieve the first milestone of this thread, you gain the following benefits.

Storyteller’s Knack. You learn three languages of your choice.

Performer’s Reward. If you have at least 3 days of downtime between adventures, you can earn GP equal to 10 times your character level by performing your stories in taverns, on the street, or for powerful patrons. Additional downtime events don’t increase this reward.

Your stories don’t just recount events . . . they shape history.

Storytellers are revered in the Northlands. Whether a skald sharing tales around a fire, a lore keeper gathering sagas to pass down the generations, or a grizzled reaver frightening young warriors by recalling harrowing battles, the tradition of collecting stories is respected across nearly all cultures in the region. As a Herald, you are a proud, if temporary, part of this tradition, driven by a need to spread stories during your travels to accomplish a specific end.

Perhaps your stories encourage downtrodden karls to rise up against their cruel jarl, warn a region about an approaching invasion, or convince warriors to join your cause. Part skald, part herald, part envoy, you will use your tales and stories to sway the minds of your audience and help shape the world.

Goals. Work with your GM to create a goal specific to your campaign or use one from the Herald Goals table. The specific nature of each milestone is dependent on the goal selected and should be agreed upon by both player and GM. Use the suggested milestone benefits described in this section or create new ones based on the rules for creating a thread.

GM Note. This thread brings flavorful Northlands themes into a campaign and can be taken up by characters of any class. However, it can tread on the toes of any Bards in the party. Consult with your Bard players before allowing a character to take on this thread to make sure no feelings will be hurt.

Herald Goals
d6 Goal
1 The feared invasion from Jotunheim approaches, and the country must be warned.
2 By telling the sagas, I will gather support for the greatest raid the North has ever launched.
3 My tales flatter Thor and Wotan in the hope they will support our struggle against the forces of Chernobog and Angrboda.
4 I spread the word of the great crusade to eradicate the darkness beneath Tanserhall. Heed my call!
5 I seek a lost treasure of Hyperborea, and by spreading tales of similar treasures, I hope to find a path to a hidden vault.
6 To rouse the people against their evil king, I shall assemble an array of sorrowful tales of corruption that cannot be ignored.

options.label: Herald (Level 5)
options.description:
Lesser Milestone Feature

When you achieve this level of milestone, you gain the following benefit.

Enthralling Speaker. You gain proficiency in the Performance skill. If you are already proficient, you instead gain Expertise. Additionally, while performing before an audience, you can cast either Charm Person or Suggestion as part of your performance without requiring somatic or material components. Use Charisma as your spellcasting ability score. After casting one of these spells using this feature, you can’t use the feature again until you finish a Long Rest.

Persuasive Words. If you make a Charisma check to change a creature's attitude or convince them of your cause, you may treat a roll of 9 or lower on the d20 as a 10. You can do this a number of times equal to your Charisma modifier per Long Rest.

options.description:
Superior Milestone Feature

When you achieve this level of milestone, you gain one of the following benefits.

Heroic Saga. Your tales grow so powerful that they can briefly tamper with luck. After you spend at least 10 minutes telling an inspiring story or legend, choose either one creature who can hear you, or up to three creatures. You can’t select yourself as a target. Once you use this feature, you can’t use it again until for 7 days.

Depending on the number of targets, creatures can receive one of the following.

Luck Boon (Three Targets). Once before the next Long Rest, a target can expend their boon to gain Advantage on a saving throw, attack roll, or ability check.

Wyrd Boon (One Target). Once before their next Long Rest, if the target does not succeed on a saving throw or attack roll, they can choose to succeed instead. An attack roll succeeding in this way results in the minimum needed to succeed (not a Critical Hit, for example).

Song of Matrimony. A member of the royal family in a particular land has fallen in love with you and your performances, and the feeling is mutual. You are engaged to marry into the royal family.
