options.label: Legend Hunter (Level 1)
options.description:
Least Milestone Feature

When you achieve the first milestone of this thread, you gain the following benefit.

Predator’s Mark. You have Advantage on Wisdom (Survival) checks made to track creatures, and on a failed check it only takes you half the time to reacquire the tracks (5 minutes in a confined area, or half an hour outdoors).

Neither storm nor night nor bloody fang shall slow your relentless pursuit.

Hunting is as old as the Northlands itself. From volcanic peaks to frigid fens and aurora-bathed glaciers, the tales of hunters are sung alongside those of heroes and gods. While some may hunt for survival or vengeance, you are different. You are a hunter to be feared, as you hunt for the thrill of the chase and the glory of victory.

As a Legend Hunter, you may have been chosen by the Norns, blessed by a god, or forged in trials of blood and grift. No matter how you come to it, your extraordinary search for greater and greater prey sets you apart from those who hunt merely for survival. You do not merely forge trails; you craft sagas. You do not merely slay beasts; you end legends to become a legend.

Goals. Work with your GM to create a goal specific to your campaign or use one from the Legend Hunter Goals table. The specific nature of each milestone is dependent on the goal selected and should be agreed upon by both player and GM. Use the suggested milestone benefits described in this section or create new ones based on the rules for creating a thread.

Legend Hunter Goals
d6 Goal
1 I will prove my valor by tracking down and slaying a legendary monster that has been destroying villages.
2 I hunt for a mythical two-headed jotun somewhere in the Bleak Expanse.
3 Besting a great dragon in its lair shall be my ultimate victory.
4 I will go beneath the waves to bring low the mother of all krakens.
5 I will bring back the head of Fenris.
6 My legend will be assured when I go among the roots of Yggdrasil to slay Nidhogg.

options.label: Legend Hunter (Level 5)
options.description:
Lesser Milestone Feature

When you achieve this level of milestone, you gain your choice of one of the following benefits.

Unclouded Sight. Your honed senses let you ignore obscurement caused by weather, such as rain, snow, or fog. This includes magical clouds and weather effects, such as Fog Cloud and Sleet Storm, but not Darkness.

Reliable Senses. After failing an Intelligence (Investigation), Wisdom (Perception), or Wisdom (Survival) check, you can reroll the check and take the higher result. Once you use this feature, you can’t use it again until you finish a Long Rest.

Least Milestone Feature

options.description:

When you achieve this level of milestone, you gain the following benefit.

Finish the Fight. When you cause a creature to become Bloodied, your next attack that hits it becomes a Critical Hit. Once you use this feature, you can’t use it again until you finish a Long Rest.

When you achieve this level of milestone, you gain your choice of one of the following benefits.

options.label: Legend Hunter (Level 17)
options.description:
Superior Milestone Feature

When you achieve this level of milestone, you gain the following benefit.

Legend Breaker. When you hit a creature that has Legendary Resistance or Legendary Actions, you can choose to forgo dealing damage to force that creature to make a Constitution saving throw (DC 8 plus your Strength or Wisdom modifier and your Proficiency Bonus). On a failed save, the target becomes unable use its Legendary Resistance or its Legendary Actions. The target can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on a success. Once you use this feature, you can’t use it again until you finish a Long Rest.

The Ultimate Favor. You performed a service for a powerful healer, who was caught in an animal trap set by another hunter. In return, they have offered to cast a single spell of healing of 6th to 9th level on you or a creature of your choice, at a time of your choosing, even if this puts them at great risk. This should be a spell that heals, regenerates, resurrects, or that removes conditions or curses.

options.label: Sworn Huskarl (Level 1)
options.description:
Least Milestone Feature

When you achieve the first milestone of this thread, you gain the following benefit.

Jarl’s Authority. The authority granted you by your loyal service can sway the opinions of others. Before rolling an Intimidation or Persuasion check to influence someone who knows and respects (or fears) your ruler, you can choose to treat the d20 roll as if you’d rolled a natural 15. Once you use this feature, you can’t use it again until you finish a Long Rest.

You carry out the will of your king, no matter the risk.

You have pledged your loyalty to an important personage, promising to guard them or travel across the Northlands in their service, risking danger as you seek to accomplish a great task. In the past, it was expected that a huskarl would swear to serve a lord for life or until they were dismissed. While jarls still take huskarls in that way, they also accept the service of huskarls on a temporary basis, to perform specific deeds or until certain threats are dealt with. These are the types of huskarls described in this thread.

The sworn huskarl enjoys a position of respect within their lord’s retinue. More than a mere bodyguard, you are a trusted agent acting on your ruler’s behalf. You carry some of their authority with you, but that also comes with the responsibility to act in ways they would approve. Sworn huskarls who bring shame to their jarl don’t remain in service for long.

A jarl takes on a new huskarl with a simple ceremony in which the huskarl swears an oath in the longhouse before a gathering of other members of the felag. During the oath-swearing, the goals or timeframe for the service are mentioned (unless the jarl keeps your mission a secret). Traditionally, your reasons for serving are respect for the lord, payment, or power. However, in some cases your oath may hold a secret. Perhaps you made it under threat of violence or under false pretenses. Or maybe your lord doesn’t deserve such loyalty. You might serve under protest or with your own goal that’s unknown to your ruler.

Goals. Work with your GM to create a goal specific to your campaign or use one from the Sworn Huskarl Goals table. The specific nature of each milestone is dependent on the goal selected and should be agreed upon by both player and GM. Use the suggested milestone benefits described in this section or create new ones based on the rules for creating a thread.

Sworn Huskarl Goals
d6 Goal
1 I will find my king’s long-lost child so that my kingdom will have a true heir.
2 My queen depends on me to find and slay the pretender who claims a blood right to her throne.
3 I must root out a hidden sect of the Cult of Ragnarok that grows within my jarl’s land.
4 Giant raiding parties threaten the land. My jarl calls upon me to destroy them and make the land safe once more.
5 I can only earn my freedom by finding the stolen crown of my sadistic monarch.
6 I serve only until I can discover a way to overthrow this corrupt king, who took the throne through dark sorcery.

options.label: Sworn Huskarl (Level 5)
options.description:
Lesser Milestone Feature

When you achieve this level of milestone, you gain your choice of one of the following benefits.

Extreme Loyalty. Your dedication to your ruler protects you from the control of others.

If you end your turn with the Charmed condition, you may choose to take Psychic damage equal to your character level, then end that condition. Once you use this feature, you can’t use it again until you finish a Long Rest.

Jarl’s Gift. To help you carry out their will, your ruler gives you your choice of one of the following uncommon magic items (or another you and your GM agree upon): Bracers of Archery, Defender’s Honey, Sentinel Shield, +1 Wand of the War Mage, or +1 Weapon.

When you are reduced to 0 Hit Points, you can use this feature to instead set your Hit Points to be equal to your character level. This doesn’t help you against an effect that would kill you instantly without dealing damage. Once you use this feature, you can’t use it again for 7 days.

Grand Reward. The completion of this milestone earns you a powerful reward. Work with your GM to determine an appropriate Very Rare magic item, such as one of the following: +3 Weapon, Dancing Sword, Oathbow, Staff of Thunder and Lightning, or Horn of the Hrimthursalr (see Chapter 5).

Additionally, while your algiz rune is active, you can end your rune early as a reaction to gain a bonus to an initiative roll equal to your Proficiency Bonus.

options.label: Shape-Shift
options.description:
As an action, you can shape-shift to change your appearance and your voice. You determine the specifics of the changes, including your coloration, hair length, and sex. You can also adjust your height and weight and can change your size between Medium and Small. You can make yourself appear as a member of another playable species, though none of your game statistics change. You can’t duplicate the appearance of an individual you’ve never seen, and you must adopt a form that has the same basic arrangement of limbs that you have. This trait doesn’t change your clothing and equipment.

While shape-shifted with this trait, you have Advantage on Charisma checks.

You stay in the new form until you take an action to revert to your true form.

This trait doesn’t change your clothing and equipment.

While shape-shifted with this trait, you have Advantage on Charisma checks.

You stay in the new form until you take an action to revert to your true form.
race.snippet:
As an action, you can shape-shift to change your appearance and your voice. You determine the specifics of the changes, including your coloration, hair length, and sex. You can also adjust your height and weight and can change your size between Medium and Small. You can make yourself appear as a member of another playable species, though none of your game statistics change. You can’t duplicate the appearance of an individual you’ve never seen, and you must adopt a form that has the same basic arrangement of limbs that you have. This trait doesn’t change your clothing and equipment.

You stay in the new form until you take an action to revert to your true form.

class.name: Unarmed Strike
class.description:
Your practice of martial arts gives you mastery of combat styles that use your Unarmed Strike and Monk weapons, which are the following:

  • Simple Melee weapons
  • Martial Melee weapons that have the [wprop]Light[/wprop] property

You gain the following benefits while you are unarmed or wielding only Monk weapons and you aren’t wearing armor or wielding a Shield.

Bonus Unarmed Strike. You can make an [rules]Unarmed Strike[/rules] as a Bonus Action.

Martial Arts Die. You can roll 1d6 in place of the normal damage of your Unarmed Strike or Monk weapons. This die changes as you gain Monk levels, as shown in the Martial Arts column of the Monk Features table.

Dexterous Attacks. You can use your Dexterity modifier instead of your Strength modifier for the attack and damage rolls of your Unarmed Strikes and Monk weapons. In addition, when you use the Grapple or Shove option of your Unarmed Strike, you can use your Dexterity modifier instead of your Strength modifier to determine the save DC.
class.snippet: You can make an Unarmed Strike as a Bonus Action, while unarmed or wielding only Monk weapons, provided you aren’t wearing armor or wielding a Shield.

class.name: Focus Points
class.description:
Your focus and martial training allow you to harness a well of extraordinary energy within yourself. This energy is represented by Focus Points. Your Monk level determines the number of points you have, as shown in the Focus Points column of the Monk Features table.

You can expend these points to enhance or fuel certain Monk features. You start knowing three such features: Flurry of Blows, Patient Defense, and Step of the Wind, each of which is detailed below.

When you expend a Focus Point, it is unavailable until you finish a Short or Long Rest, at the end of which you regain all your expended points.

Some features that use Focus Points require your target to make a saving throw. The save DC equals 8 plus your Wisdom modifier and Proficiency Bonus.

Flurry of Blows. You can expend 1 Focus Point to make two Unarmed Strikes as a Bonus Action.

Patient Defense. You can take the [action]Disengage[/action] action as a Bonus Action. Alternatively, you can expend 1 Focus Point to take both the [action]Disengage[/action] and the [action]Dodge[/action] actions as a Bonus Action.

Step of the Wind. You can take the [action]Dash[/action] action as a Bonus Action. Alternatively, you can expend 1 Focus Point to take both the [action]Disengage[/action] and [action]Dash[/action] actions as a Bonus Action, and your jump distance is doubled for the turn.
class.snippet: You can expend Focus Points to enhance or fuel certain Monk features. You have {{classlevel}} Focus Points and regain all expended points after you finish a Short or Long Rest.

class.name: Uncanny Metabolism
class.description:
When you roll [rules]Initiative[/rules], you can regain all expended Focus Points. When you do so, roll your Martial Arts die, and regain a number of Hit Points equal to your Monk level plus the number rolled.

Once you use this feature, you can’t use it again until you finish a [rules]Long Rest[/rules].
class.snippet: Once per Long Rest, when you roll Initiative you can regain all expended Focus Points, and regain {{scalevalue}}{{classlevel#signed}} HP.

class.name: Deflect Attack
class.description:
When an attack roll hits you and its damage includes Bludgeoning, Piercing, or Slashing damage, you can take a Reaction to reduce the attack’s total damage against you. The reduction equals 1d10 plus your Dexterity modifier and Monk level.

If you reduce the damage to 0, you can expend 1 Focus Point to redirect some of the attack’s force. If you do so, choose a creature you can see within 5 feet of yourself if the attack was a melee attack or a creature you can see within 60 feet of yourself that isn’t behind [rules]Total Cover[/rules] if the attack was a ranged attack. That creature must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw or take damage equal to two rolls of your Martial Arts die plus your Dexterity modifier. The damage is the same type dealt by the attack.
class.snippet: When an attack roll that includes Bludgeoning, Piercing, or Slashing damage hits you, you can reduce the attack’s total damage by 1d10{{(modifier:dex+classlevel)#signed}}.

class.name: Deflect Attack: Redirect Attack
class.description:
When an attack roll hits you and its damage includes Bludgeoning, Piercing, or Slashing damage, you can take a Reaction to reduce the attack’s total damage against you. The reduction equals 1d10 plus your Dexterity modifier and Monk level.

If you reduce the damage to 0, you can expend 1 Focus Point to redirect some of the attack’s force. If you do so, choose a creature you can see within 5 feet of yourself if the attack was a melee attack or a creature you can see within 60 feet of yourself that isn’t behind [rules]Total Cover[/rules] if the attack was a ranged attack. That creature must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw or take damage equal to two rolls of your Martial Arts die plus your Dexterity modifier. The damage is the same type dealt by the attack.
class.snippet: If you reduce the damage to 0 via Deflect Attacks, you can expend 1 Focus Point to redirect some of the attack’s force. If you do, choose a creature you can see within 5 ft. of you if it was melee, or a creature you can see within 60 ft. that isn’t behind Total Cover if the attack was ranged. The creature must succeed on a DC {{savedc:wis}} Dex. saving throw or take {{scalevalue}}{{modifier:dex#signed}} damage of the same type dealt by the attack.

class.name: Slow Fall
class.description: You can take a Reaction when you fall to reduce any damage you take from the fall by an amount equal to five times your Monk level.
class.snippet: When you fall, you can reduce any falling damage by {{5*classlevel#unsigned}}.

class.name: Stunning Strike
class.description: Once per turn when you hit a creature with a Monk weapon or an Unarmed Strike, you can expend 1 Focus Point to attempt a stunning strike. The target must make a Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, the target has the [condition]Stunned[/condition] condition until the start of your next turn. On a successful save, the target’s Speed is halved until the start of your next turn, and the next attack roll made against the target before then has Advantage.
class.snippet: Once per turn, when you hit with a Monk weapon or Unarmed Strike, you can expend 1 Focus Point to attempt a stunning strike. The target must make a DC {{savedc:wis}} Con. saving throw. On a failed save, the target has the Stunned condition until the start of your next turn. On a successful save, the target’s Speed is halved until the start of your next turn, and the next attack roll made against it has Advantage.

class.name: Empowered Strikes
class.description: Whenever you deal damage with your Unarmed Strike, it can deal your choice of Force damage or its normal damage type.
class.snippet: Whenever you deal damage with your Unarmed Strike, it can deal your choice of Force damage or its normal damage type.

class.name: Flurry of Blows: Addle
class.description:
Whenever you hit a creature with an attack granted by your Flurry of Blows, you can impose one of the following effects on that target.

Addle. The target can’t make [action]Opportunity Attack;Opportunity Attacks[/action] until the start of its next turn.

Push. The target must succeed on a Strength saving throw or be pushed up to 15 feet away from you.

Topple. The target must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw or have the [condition]Prone[/condition] condition.
class.snippet: When you hit a creature with an attack granted by Flurry of Blows, the target can’t make Opportunity Attacks until the start of its next turn.

class.name: Wholeness of Body
class.description:
You gain the ability to heal yourself. As a Bonus Action, you can roll your Martial Arts die. You regain a number of Hit Points equal to the number rolled plus your Wisdom modifier (minimum of 1 Hit Point regained).

You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum of once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a [rules]Long Rest[/rules].
class.snippet:
You can heal yourself for {{scalevalue}}{{modifier:wis@min:0#signed}} HP. 

You can use this feature {{modifier:wis@min:1#unsigned}} times per Long Rest.

feat.name: Enhanced Unarmed Strike
feat.description: When you hit with your Unarmed Strike and deal damage, you can deal Bludgeoning damage equal to 1d4 plus your Strength modifier instead of the normal damage of an Unarmed Strike.
feat.snippet: When you hit with your Unarmed Strike and deal damage, you can deal 1d4{{modifier:str#signed}} Bludgeoning damage instead of the normal damage of an Unarmed Strike.

feat.name: Push
feat.description: When you hit a creature with an Unarmed Strike as part of the Attack action on your turn, you can deal damage to the target and also push it 5 feet away from you. You can use this benefit only once per turn.
feat.snippet: Once per turn, when you hit a creature with an Unarmed Strike, you can deal damage to the target and also push it 5 ft. away from you.

feat.name: Echoing Soul: Intrusive Echoes
feat.description: Immediately after you make a [rules]D20 Test[/rules] and roll a 1 on the d20, memories and sensations from your soul’s other life threaten to overtake you. Make a Constitution saving throw (DC 13 plus your Proficiency Bonus). On a failed save, you have the [condition]Incapacitated[/condition] condition until the end of your next turn. While you are Incapacitated in this way, your Speed is halved.
feat.snippet: Immediately after you make a D20 Test and roll a 1 on the d20, make a Con. saving throw (DC {{13+proficiency#unsigned}}). On a failed save, you have the Incapacitated condition until the end of your next turn. While Incapacitated in this way, your Speed is halved.
